name: Remove QoS Reserved Bandwidth Limit
description: By default Windows reserves 20% of bandwidth for QoS/DSCP traffic. Removing this reservation allows all apps to use the full connection speed.

name: Disable IPv6
description: Disables IPv6 on all network adapters. Removes the IPv6 attack surface and can reduce DNS lookup delays on networks without IPv6 infrastructure. Reboot required.

name: Disable Network Location Pop-up
description: Suppresses the 'Do you want your PC to be discoverable?' pop-up that appears every time you connect to a new network.

name: Optimise DNS Cache TTL
description: Sets the DNS client cache max TTL to 86400 s (24 h) and negative cache to 300 s. Reduces repeat DNS lookups for frequently visited domains.

name: Disable Network Discovery (SSDP / UPnP)
description: Stops the SSDP Discovery, UPnP Host, and Function Discovery services that broadcast this machine's presence on the LAN. Reduces attack surface on public or untrusted networks.

name: Disable LMHosts Name Resolution
description: Disables the legacy LMHosts file used for NetBIOS name resolution. No modern application relies on this — disabling it reduces NetBIOS query broadcast noise.

name: Shorten TCP KeepAlive Interval
description: Reduces the TCP KeepAlive interval from 2 hours to 5 minutes. Detects dead connections faster, which improves resilience for apps that hold long-lived TCP sockets.

name: Disable TCP Timestamps
description: Disables the TCP timestamp option (RFC 1323). Timestamps can expose system uptime to network peers and marginally increase per-packet overhead. Low security improvement; mainly for hardening.
